Front: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters: what do you do on a duplicate, and why is it O(n)?
Back: Grow the window by moving r; when s[r] is already in the set (seen), SHRINK from the left - remove s[l], l+=1 - until the duplicate is gone, then add s[r]; track the max window size. Do NOT reset the window. O(n) because l and r each only move forward and every char enters/leaves the set at most once (amortized), so the inner while is not multiplicative. Use a set, not a fixed 26-array (input has spaces/digits/symbols).

Front: Shadow release vs canary release (ML model rollout).
Back: Shadow: the new model runs on real live traffic in parallel, but its outputs are DISCARDED - no user ever sees them (zero user risk; cost is double compute and no outcome signal). Canary: a small slice of real users actually RECEIVE the new model's outputs, so you get real outcome data but carry user-facing risk on that slice.

Front: Rolling update vs self-healing: how many ReplicaSets, and what happens?
Back: Rolling update (image/spec change): TWO ReplicaSets - the Deployment scales the NEW one up while scaling the OLD one down, gradually; the overlap (old pods serve until new are Ready) gives zero downtime; old kept at 0 for rollback. Self-healing (a pod dies): ONE ReplicaSet recreates the same-version pod to restore the desired count. Ask 'how many ReplicaSets?' to tell them apart.

Front: In a Deployment YAML, selector.matchLabels does not match template.metadata.labels. What happens on kubectl apply?
Back: Kubernetes REJECTS the Deployment at apply time with a validation error ('selector does not match template labels') - it is never created. Not a silent 'won't connect.' A Deployment must be able to select the very pods it creates. (Routing/connect language applies to Services, not this.)
